title: Referral fraud flag catalogue
description: Every valid flag_type + the expected `details` JSONB shape + suggested severity + suggested confidence-scoring inputs. The ReferralFraudFlagObserver validates details.shape against this catalogue at create time.

flag_types.description: The referrer and referred share an IP hash within a 90-day window. Strong signal of self-referral or same-household refund abuse.
Referrer + referred share a device fingerprint hash. Same-device self-referral signal.
Referred email domain matches the known-disposable list (data/disposable-email-domains.json).
Referred email is a plus-addressing variant of the referrer's email (alice@example.com + alice+ref@example.com).
The referrer submitted > max_referrals_per_hour in the last 60 min.
> 10 claims from the same IP in 10 min without subsequent signups. Suggests scraper / bot activity.
Referred email domain is in the tenant's per-program blacklist (fraud_config.blacklist_domains).
Referrer + referred IP geolocation differs by > 5000km. VPN abuse signal — not conclusive on its own.

Composite heuristic combining several soft signals (rapid claim -> signup -> trigger; no in-app engagement between events; abnormal timezone drift). No single sub-signal fires alone; the composite score does.

severity_defaults.warn: Creates a flag; referral proceeds. Ops can review later.
severity_defaults.block: Creates a flag; referral proceeds only if fraud_score is below hard_block_threshold. Blocks vesting until admin reviews.
severity_defaults.critical: Creates a flag; parent Referral immediately transitions to status='fraudulent'. Cascades rewards to clawback.

notes: flag_type + severity + confidence + details are IMMUTABLE post-create. Only disposition + reviewed_* fields mutate.
The ReferralFraudFlagObserver validates the `details` JSONB shape at create time against this catalogue.
Every fraud flag is retained 7 years hard (no soft-delete) per SOC 2 + ISO 27001 fraud audit trail retention.

Adding a new flag_type requires: (1) update this catalogue; (2) implement the rule in FraudDetector; (3) update the ReferralFraudFlagType enum cast; (4) add SDUI copy for the new type; (5) migration to widen the flag_type enum in Postgres.
